meaning: to be there; to exist; to have

expressions.english: to be at home
to have a book
to have money
to stay still, not move
expressions.notes:
가만히 = still; motionlessly
가만히 있다 = to stay still, not move

expressions.english: to have a class (to attend/teach)
to have plans; to have someone to meet; to have somewhere to go
Is there a problem?; Is there anything wrong?; What's going on?
Do you have money to go on a trip?
expressions.notes:
여행 = travel, trip
가다 = to go; to leave

expressions.english: Were you at home yesterday?
There is an air conditioner in the living room.
expressions.notes:
거실 = living room
에어컨 = air conditioner

expressions.english: There is someone I know.
There is an eraser on the desk.
expressions.notes:
책상 = desk
위 = above; on; up
지우개 = an eraser

expressions.english:
I think there is someone in there.
I think someone's in there.

expressions.korean: 우리 집에는 방이 세 개 있어요.
expressions.english: There are three rooms in my house.
expressions.notes:
우리 = we; us
집 = house, home
우리 집 = my house
방 = room
세 = three (native Korean number)
개 = a general counter for inanimate objects.

expressions.english: Don't move and stay still.
There is ice cream in the refrigerator.
Stay here for a second. I will be right back.
expressions.notes:
잠깐만 = short time; for a moment
-만 = only; just
여기 = here, this place
돌아오다 = to return; to come back
-(으)ㄹ게 것이다 = will; be going to + V

expressions.korean: 오늘은 약속이 있어서 먼저 가 봐야 돼요.
expressions.english: I have to go somewhere today, so I'm going to leave first.

expressions.korean: 저희 집은 여기에서 가까운 곳에 있어 요.
expressions.english: My house is near here.
expressions.notes:
저희 = (polite) we; us; our
저희 집 = (polite) my house
가깝다 = to be near, close (in distance)
가까운 = is the adjective form of 가깝다.

expressions.korean: 저는 3시에 수업이 있어서 지금 가 봐 야 돼요.
expressions.english: I have a class at three, so I have to go now.
expressions.notes:
3시 = three o'clock
가 보다 = to leave (due to a previous engagement or time restriction)
-이/어/(아) 되다 = to have to + V
